mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform for serving large language models (LLMs) like Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5, and others. It simplifies the deployment and management of these models on Kubernetes, making it ideal for developers looking to integrate AI capabilities into their applications. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate resources and potential complexity in managing model versions.

steps.title: Add Ollama Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Ollama Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Ollama using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add ollama https://ollama.com/helm

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the Ollama repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Ollama CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Ollama to function correctly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://ollama.com/crds/ollama-crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Ollama Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama application on your Kubernetes cluster using the specified version.

```bash

helm install ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama --create-namespace

```

> Installs Ollama in the 'ollama' namespace.

steps.title: Create an Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example custom resource to deploy a model using Ollama.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ollama.com/v1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: gemma3
spec:
  model: gemma3
EOF

```

> This command creates a model resource for Gemma 3.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ollama.com/v1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: gemma3
spec:
  model: gemma3
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Ollama Installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Ollama deployment and ensures that the model is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n ollama

kubectl get models -n ollama

```

> This command lists the pods in the 'ollama' namespace.
> This command checks the status of the deployed models.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ollama installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Note that this will delete all associated resources, including models and data.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

kubectl delete namespace ollama

```

> Uninstalls the Ollama Helm release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your models and configurations. This step will upgrade Ollama to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get models -n ollama -o yaml > backup-models.yaml

helm upgrade ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama

kubectl get pods -n ollama

```

> Backup current models.
> Upgrades the Ollama installation.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get models -n ollama -o yaml > backup-models.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the image repository is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure that the repository is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n ollama' and adjust resource limits if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Model not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified model may not be deployed or the resource definition is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the model resource is correctly defined and deployed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient permissions for the service account used by Ollama.

**Fix:**
Check and update the RBAC permissions for the Ollama service account.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models including Kimi-K2.5 and improved REST API endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n ollama' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ollama.com/v1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: gemma3
spec:
  model: gemma3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
